Description{Enhanced}: It only took a few heated skirmishes with the allied city–states for Garlemald to realize that the magicks wielded by the realm's mages were too much for even the thickest of magitek armor plating. Alloyed gilding has since been discovered to dampen the effects of elemental charges and is now standard issue.

It only took a few heated skirmishes with the allied city–states for Garlemald to realize that the magicks wielded by the realm's mages were too much for even the thickest of magitek armor plating. Alloyed gilding has since been discovered to dampen the effects of elemental charges and is now standard-issue.

Description: Summon forth the pilgrim's protector, a wandering worshiper's best friend.
Description{Enhanced}: A white-furred beast native to lands formerly belonging to the Kingdom of Voeburt. They can be domesticated if raised from birth, and were known to escort adherents of the Church of the First Light on pilgrimages. Due to its exceptional docility, this specimen is believed to be descended from such trained creatures.
Tooltip:
There is much I could learn
from your pilgrimage.
   - Hien

Description: Summon forth Vurgar the Loyal, and give your enemies twenty seconds to comply.
Description{Enhanced}: In ages past, the Yok Huy employed golems such as this as weapons of war, but following Tuliyollal's founding, they refrained from creating new ones out of their newfound aversion to conflict. In the Yok Huy tongue, the name Vurgar simply means “one who possesses a body of stone.”
Tooltip:
If you want the golem restored,
you may leave it to my husband!
   - Dulia-Chai

Description: Summon forth your forgiven mimicry. Not actually a sin eater, so it can be ridden without fear of turning those around you into one.
Description{Enhanced}: Found prowling Pilgrim's Traverse, this motley creature appeared to be a sin eater at first glance, but is in fact a variety of ambulating plant. It is believed that it learned to assume its present form in order to survive in the Light-flooded First.
Tooltip:
I don't think anyone can truly
mimic your unique strengths.
   - Jessie

Description: Summon forth your Seikret, the steed of choice for serious hunters.
Description{Enhanced}: A beast of burden known for its strength and swiftness. Additionally possessed of a superior sense of smell, they are able to track prey over great distances, making them an indispensable hunting companion whence they hail.
Tooltip:
So what's the adventure?
Monster hunting?
   - Yozan

Description: Summon forth your Felyne support team cart, safe in the knowledge that there is no reward penalty for riding it.
Description{Enhanced}: Featuring a time-tested construction, this cart is purpose-built to bear distressed hunters to safety. No matter how dangerous the destination, the Felynes will never fail to sally forth to the rescue, but it appears some hunters would not be caught dead being carted away.

Description: Summon forth your chandelier of First Light, and live like tomorrow doesn't exist. Like it doesn't exist.
Description{Enhanced}: A furnishing at the Church of the First Light, this chandelier was imbued with levitation magicks to prevent it from falling and causing injury. In a development the church could not have foreseen, it has since been enhanced by fae enchantments and made able to soar the skies while bearing a rider.
Tooltip:
My, how fancy. Though I suppose
this is a rather fancy place, isn't it.
   - Moogle Suitor

Description: Summon forth your ornamental shrublet. Seeks either revenge or proliferation or both on its way to a new land.
Description{Enhanced}: This ambulating plant appears to be an offshoot of the ornamental shrubs you encountered along Pilgrim's Traverse. Though it is unclear whether it desires revenge for its fallen parents or simply wishes to sow its seeds in faraway lands, it has seemingly decided to follow you.
Tooltip:
Only the most fearsome
bits of shrubbery get given
elite mark status.
   - Aytienne
